steps.title: Set up the total weight expression
steps.content:
The truck carries three types of load: a 190-pound piece of equipment, crates weighing 25 pounds each (where x is the count), and crates weighing 62 pounds each (where y is the count). The total weight is:

$$\text{Total weight} = 190 + 25x + 62y$$

steps.title: Apply the capacity constraint and simplify
steps.content:
The total weight cannot exceed the truck's maximum capacity of 5,630 pounds, so:

$$190 + 25x + 62y \le 5,630$$

Subtract 190 from both sides:

$$25x + 62y \le 5,440$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
